title: Lesson 236
transcript: SLA's, Service Level Agreements. These are the agreements that organizations have in place with their service providers. And these agreements define the contractual requirements that are in place so that service providers know exactly what they're supposed to be doing and how they're supposed to be doing it. and maybe not doing stuff like this. So service level agreements specifically define the different service levels, performance levels that are expected in the contract or that are expected to be provided. They define the governance that should be in place to make sure that every party knows who is responsible for what, the consumer and the service provider. The SLA will define the security controls that the organization expects the service provider to have in place. And this very importantly relates to a concept we've talked about already, which is accountability and responsibility. An organization cannot outsource its accountability. It can only outsource responsibility. And what that very clearly means, and this is why SLAs are so important, A system or data owner that has accountability for protecting data, they can't just outsource that accountability to the service provider. Instead, what they have to do is very clearly define the controls that the service provider needs to have in place to appropriately protect the data and that way the business owner the data owner that's accountable for the data can make sure that their service providers are actually doing what they need to be doing and that accountable person can actually make sure that they're not gonna they're not gonna throw it that's gonna get choked if something goes wrong These SLA documents also include components around achievement of metrics and identification of issues, essentially reporting. So how does a business owner actually make sure that their service provider is meeting the SLA is actually doing what they're supposed to be doing. Well, the way this works is the SLA contains components around reporting back to the business owners of this customer that here's how we're meeting the requirements of the SLA. Now you've probably heard terms like, well we've certainly talked about SLA, but you've heard of probably contracts and maybe even PLAs, privacy level agreements. How do all of these things fit together? Well, SLAs and PLAs are addendums to the contract. The contract contains all the overall business terms, price of the agreement, all those, you know, what happens if we want to back out of the agreement. All those sort of business terms are contained in the contract and the SLA, and if there is one, PLA are addendums to that contract. And let's tie in one final piece that we've talked about, and that is SOC 2 reports. The primary way that most consumers get assurance that their service providers are doing what they're supposed to be doing is not through having a custom SLA. Because for a lot of service providers out there, think cloud, the big cloud service providers usually will not provide custom SLAs. And so if a consumer really wants to get assurance that their provider is doing what they need them to do, typically that assurance will come through a SOC report. And of course the type of SOC report we want is a SOC 2 Type 2.
